title: llama.cpp
oneLiner: Portable local inference for open-weight language models.
summary: llama.cpp is a fast, portable inference stack for running open-weight language models across local machines, servers, and edge devices.
whyItMatters: Local inference is one of the most important building blocks for open AI. llama.cpp gives builders a practical way to run models without depending on a hosted API.
notFor: Teams that only want hosted APIs
Projects that need managed model operations
seoTitle: llama.cpp: Open-source local inference for AI models
seoDescription: A concise editorial profile of llama.cpp, including what it does, who it is best for, license, repo, and related open-source AI resources.
shareDescription: Portable local inference for open-weight language models.
featuredReason: A foundational local inference project for the open AI stack.

coreStrengths.title: Portable local inference
coreStrengths.description: llama.cpp focuses on running open-weight language models across local machines, servers, and edge-style environments.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: It reduces dependency on hosted model APIs when builders need local control, privacy, or offline-friendly experimentation.

coreStrengths.title: Open-weight model ecosystem support
coreStrengths.description: The project is closely associated with local workflows around GGUF and the broader open model ecosystem.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: That makes it a common infrastructure layer for tools that need to run models outside a managed cloud platform.

coreStrengths.title: Low-level inference building block
coreStrengths.description: Rather than being a polished app, llama.cpp is a foundation that other tools and workflows can build around.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: This is valuable for developers who want control over model execution, deployment targets, and performance tradeoffs.

useCaseNotes.title: Local model experiments
useCaseNotes.description: Use it to test open-weight models on your own machine before committing to a hosted setup.

useCaseNotes.title: Edge or private inference
useCaseNotes.description: A fit when model execution needs to happen near the user, near the data, or outside a third-party API.

useCaseNotes.title: Infrastructure for local AI apps
useCaseNotes.description: Useful as a lower-level engine behind chat UIs, developer tools, and self-hosted AI workflows.

compareNotes.title: Choose it when local control matters
compareNotes.summary: Hosted APIs are simpler operationally, but llama.cpp is stronger when control, portability, or local execution are the main requirements.

compareNotes.title: Expect infrastructure work
compareNotes.summary: It is a powerful building block, not a turnkey hosted product. Teams should be ready to own model setup and deployment details.

thumbnailBrief.backgroundStyle: clean technical surface with high contrast model blocks
